intro: Settlement currency is the currency of cash or clearing-house movement — where liquidity friction converts negotiated obligations into bank-statement reality.
state_summary: State 03 captures wires, ACH-style rails, on-behalf payments, and netting settlements. When settlement diverges from transaction denomination, FX gains or losses begin their journey toward postings defenders must later narrate.
state_role_body: Treasury executes here daily while controllers await mirrored postings. Misalignment between banking cutoff calendars and ERP value dates produces phantom breaks controllers mistaken attribute purely to rate noise.
custody_question: Which currency actually clears cash — and does documentation tie every movement back to its originating obligation currency?
chain_position_body: Settlement bridges commercial obligation identity toward accounting custody. Weak handshake between TMS feeds and ERP cash modules fractures traceability before accounts ever assign custody identities.

state_rail.name: Transaction Currency
state_rail.description: Denomination of the recorded financial event.

state_rail.name: Settlement Currency
state_rail.description: Cash or settlement rail actually moved.

state_rail.name: Account Currency
state_rail.description: Assigned custody currency for maintained balances.

state_rail.name: Reporting Currency
state_rail.description: Presentation layer for management and filings.

state_risk_body: Partial settlements and FX netting obscure which currency truly extinguished obligation fragments — leaving account assignments guessing.
state_control_body: Pair bank confirmations with obligation references and mandate dual-sign reconciliation between treasury and accounting before period locks.
passes_to: Account currency custody once monetary movement posts into governed balances.
